description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, blocky rock with a predominantly white to light beige coloration and a rugged, uneven surface. The top face displays several shallow depressions and irregular patches of discoloration, possibly from weathering or mineral deposits. There are visible human-made markings, including a blue painted area with numbers and a small white circular label, suggesting cataloging or identification. The sides are jagged and chipped, with some areas showing a more granular texture. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and shape suggest it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or landscaped area.
description: A blocky, white-beige rock with rough, uneven surfaces and visible blue and white human-made markings. Its solid, dense form and irregular shape make it suitable as an urban obstacle or landscaping element.
description_view_0: The top and one broad side of the rock are visible, showing a mostly flat surface with shallow depressions and a blue marking near one edge.
description_view_1: This view highlights a more squared-off end with a white circular label and the blue marking partially visible, emphasizing the rock's blocky shape.
description_view_2: The opposite side reveals the blue marking more clearly and shows the rock's irregular, sloping surface with some discoloration.
description_view_3: A side view displaying the rock's height and jagged, chipped edges, with less visible marking but more pronounced surface texture.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
